items.headline: NBA top rookie Cooper Flagg gets a ride from NASCAR rookie Connor Zilisch
items.description:
FORT WORTH, Texas (AP) — NBA top rookie Cooper Flagg got a fast ride on the track Sunday before the NASCAR race in Texas with another 19-year-old, Cup Series rookie Connor Zilisch.
A week after being named the NBA Rookie of the Year, the Dallas Mavericks' 6-foot-9 forward was the honorary pace car driver for the race in Texas.

items.headline: Catcher Cal Raleigh is out of the Mariners' lineup vs. Royals again with a sore side
items.description:
SEATTLE (AP) — Seattle Mariners catcher Cal Raleigh is out of the lineup for the second straight day with a sore side, manager Dan Wilson announced Sunday.
Raleigh began experiencing soreness on his right side following Friday night’s game against the Kansas City Royals and was a late scratch from Saturday’s game.

items.headline: Joel Embiid wants 76ers fans at Philadelphia games. The Knicks know that New Yorkers are coming
items.description:
GREENBURGH, N.Y. (AP) — Joel Embiid wants Philadelphia fans at the Philadelphia games.
Good luck with that. One way or another, the Knicks know the New Yorkers are coming.

items.headline: Kimi Antonelli wins the Miami Grand Prix for his third straight F1 victory
items.description: MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — Kimi Antonelli wins the Miami Grand Prix for his third straight F1 victory.

items.headline: Kimi Antonelli wins the Miami Grand Prix for his third straight F1 victory
items.description:
MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — Kimi Antonelli made history as the first driver to win his first three Formula 1 races from the pole when the 19-year-old Mercedes driver won the Miami Grand Prix on Sunday.
It was the third consecutive victory for Antonelli, the current points leader who is proving early this season to be a legitimate championship contender.

items.headline: Wild will be without Joel Eriksson Ek and Jonas Brodin for first two games of series vs. Avs
items.description:
DENVER (AP) — The Minnesota Wild will be without forward Joel Eriksson Ek and defenseman Jonas Brodin for the first two games of their second-round series against the Colorado Avalanche because of lower-body injuries.
Neither player made the trip to Denver. Wild coach John Hynes said the team is still “making a couple decisions on the lineup" ahead of Game 1 on Sunday night.
